sglang.title: [Disagg][NIXL] Support Mamba state slice transfer for heterogeneous TP (Step 2/2 for Qwen3.5)
sglang.author: YAMY1234
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Depends on #22145.
The Mooncake backend already supports Mamba state slice transfer under heterogeneous TP (prefill TP ≠ decode TP), but NIXL lacks this capability and raises RuntimeError. This PR adds the same support for NIXL, enabling hybrid Mamba models (e.g., Qwen3.5) to run disaggre

sglang.title: [Mamba] eliminate D2H if tracking mamba states
sglang.author: Henson-Zh-Ali
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
With using Qwen3.5-397B-A17B model, we observed lots of bubbles when setting --mamba-scheduler-strategy extra_buffer. After analysis, we confirm that the bubbles originated from a D2H operation. By eliminating these bubbles, we have effectively improved TTFT (Time-To-First-Token) and TPS

sglang.title: fix: Auto-correct page_size for Mamba no_buffer radix cache mode
sglang.author: alphabetc1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
MambaRadixCache v1 (no_buffer) asserts page_size==1 at init, but server_args lacked early validation, causing a hard crash when users passed --page-size >1 with hybrid Mamba models.

sglang.title: Support HTTP2 server
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Add HTTP/2 support via Granian as an optional ASGI server (--enable-http2).
HTTP/2 multiplexing allows many in-flight requests over a single TCP connection, reducing connection overhead and improving throughput for high-concurrency clients. This is particularly useful for streaming worklo

Motivation

The NSA indexer currently launches several unnecessary kernels:

_project_and_scale_head_gates and _get_logits_head_gate use multiple element-wise kernels.
Query/key updates force clone() to avoid self-aliasing errors, which introduces extra copy kernels.

Summary

Change upload-cuda-coredumps step from if: always() to if: failure() across all CI workflows

sglang.title: [fix] Fix writer lock deadlock in update_weights_from_ipc during pause_generation
sglang.author: maocheng23
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Fix writer lock deadlock in update_weights_from_ipc when scheduler is in pause_generation mode
When the scheduler is paused, existing readers are blocked on is_pause_cond, so acquiring the writer lock would deadlock. This change skips the writer lock when paused (using nullcontext()) since

sglang.title: [AMD] Fix DLPack Error in Aiter flydsl GEMM by Detaching MoE Gate Weight
sglang.author: bingxche
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The new aiter flydsl GEMM backend exports tensors via DLPack, which raises BufferError on tensors with requires_grad=True. MoEGate.weight is an nn.Parameter (requires_grad=True by default), causing crashes during CUDA graph capture.
Detach the weight before passing to tgemm.mm. This is a

vllm.title: [ROCm] Remove unused IS_FNUZ parameter from reshape_and_cache_shuffle_kernel
vllm.author: Bortlesboat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
IS_FNUZ is declared as a tl.constexpr kernel parameter and computed at the call site via current_platform.fp8_dtype() == torch.float8_e4m3fnuz, but it's never referenced in the kernel body (lines 233-266). This means every call to reshape_and_cache_shuffle does a platform check for nothing.
Also, si

vllm.title: nano-nemotron-vl: get_mm_max_tokens_per_item for audio, video, image == seq_len
vllm.author: netanel-haber
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
For nano-nemotron-vl: Hardcode max_seq_len as the upper limit of mm items. This is a coarse way to currently sidestep the limits of the dummy audio-video profiling interface.
We allow for requests to contain dynamic FPS and dynamic max_num_frames, and see no reason to cap those statically at runtime

vllm.title: Fix Mistral yarn warning in Transformers v5

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As of huggingface/transformers#41250 the ignore_keys argument to validate_rope was removed in favour of ClassVars attached to the config classes themselves.
This PR sets this new ClassVar for Mistral models which use yarn so that the warning from Transformers RoPE validation is suppressed.
cc @julie

vllm.title: fix(test): recompute Jina ColBERT rotary inv_freq cleared by transformers v5 weight loader
vllm.author: ieBoytsov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Summary

Transformers 5.0 introduced a new weight materialization system that clears non-persistent buffers (persistent=False) during from_pretrained
The Jina ColBERT model's RotaryEmbedding registers inv_freq as a non-persistent buffer, so it gets wiped to uninitialized memory after weight

vllm.title: [KVConnector]: prioritize external connector over internal registry

Purpose
Fix the connector resolution priority in KVConnectorFactory. Previously, if an externally specified connector_name matched a name already registered in the internal registry, the internal connector would be used silently, ignoring the user-provided kv_connector_module_path. Now the external

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Quantization] Fix PerTensorScale loading with tuple shard_id in MergedColumnParallelLinear

Fix a bug(#38197) in MergedColumnParallelLinear.weight_loader_v2 where PerTensorScaleParameter loading with a tuple shard_id (e.g., (0, 1, 2)) incorrectly hardcodes shard_id=0, causing only the first scale slot to be populated. This results in corrupted weights and garbage output for models

This PR introduces the implementation of the 3FS KVConnector for vLLM.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Frontend] Fix Gemma4 streaming HTML duplication after tool calls
vllm.author: yoke233
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fix a Gemma4 streaming bug where buffered deltas are stitched back into current_text, which can corrupt normal text after or inside tool calls.
This showed up most clearly with HTML content in tool arguments such as write_file(content=...), where tags like <html> and <meta> could be streamed
